skill.name: senior-data-engineer
skill.description: Data engineering guidance for designing pipelines, data models, quality checks, and DataOps workflows with Python, Spark, Airflow, dbt, and Kafka.
skill.summary: Design production data pipelines, quality checks, and DataOps workflows.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak cryptography alerts and medium shell backtick alerts, but review found documentation headings, markdown code fences, file path references, and simple local Python CLI templates. No evidence found of shell execution, network reconnaissance, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or cryptographic implementation misuse. The skill contains runnable scripts that accept local paths, so scripts are recorded as a benign risk factor.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are documentation metadata, section headings, and general engineering terms such as design, algorithms, encryption, reliability, and performance. No hash functions, cipher APIs, key handling, or cryptographic code were found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows these alerts point to prose, headings, JSON marketplace text, or argparse descriptions. There is no nearby cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Backtick Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged shell execution locations are markdown code fences, inline code references to local files, or JSON examples containing prose. No Ruby backtick operator, subprocess call, os.system call, or command construction was found at the flagged locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SKILL.md lines are markdown delimiters, example commands, and inline file names. The JSON lines are evaluation prose, not executable Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged reconnaissance lines use phrases such as resource awareness or monitoring inside data engineering documentation. No port scanning, network probing, host discovery, or outbound network code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation and output examples about resources and monitoring. Targeted keyword review found no network libraries, URLs, or scanning behavior in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Benign Local CLI Scripts Present
low_findings.description: Verdict: LOW_RISK. The package includes three Python CLI templates that parse local input and output path arguments and print status JSON. They do not currently read files, write files, launch commands, or contact networks, but executable scripts require normal user review before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are small and their main functions only parse arguments, instantiate local classes, and print results. The low risk comes from their executable nature, not from confirmed harmful behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable pipelines, clear data models, and repeatable quality checks. This skill provides practical data engineering patterns for batch, streaming, orchestration, and DataOps work.
actual_capabilities: Explains production data pipeline architecture for batch and streaming systems.
Provides guidance for ETL and ELT workflows using Python, Spark, Airflow, dbt, and Kafka.
Outlines data quality checks, schema validation, monitoring, and alerting patterns.
Describes data modeling patterns for scalable warehouses, lakes, and feature pipelines.
Includes simple Python CLI templates for pipeline orchestration, quality validation, and performance optimization.
Covers DataOps practices such as testing, deployment discipline, observability, and incident response.
limitations: The included scripts are templates and do not implement full pipeline logic.
It does not provision cloud services, databases, clusters, or orchestration platforms.
Users must adapt examples to their data sources, credentials, schemas, and compliance needs.
It provides guidance and structure, not a managed data platform.

use_cases.title: Plan a new pipeline
use_cases.description: Design a batch or streaming pipeline with validation, orchestration, monitoring, and failure handling.

use_cases.title: Improve data models
use_cases.description: Review warehouse models, transformation patterns, testing coverage, and freshness checks for reliable analytics.

use_cases.title: Standardize DataOps practices
use_cases.description: Create shared guidance for deployment, observability, incident response, and operational ownership of pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a simple daily ETL pipeline for customer events. Include sources, transformations, storage, quality checks, and monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Data Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this warehouse model design for scalability, data quality, maintainability, and query performance. Recommend concrete improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Pipeline Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze a slow Spark pipeline. Focus on partitioning, caching, shuffle reduction, batch sizing, and operational metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Define a DataOps Standard
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DataOps standard for production pipelines. Include testing, deployment, observability, incident response, ownership, and compliance controls.

output_examples.input: Design a streaming analytics pipeline for product events.
output: Architecture with event producers, Kafka topics, stream processing, durable storage, and analytics consumers.
Operational plan covering schema checks, lag monitoring, replay strategy, and alert thresholds.
Reliability guidance for partitioning, checkpointing, retries, and dead letter handling.

output_examples.input: Improve data quality for a feature store.
output: Validation plan for schema, freshness, completeness, ranges, uniqueness, and distribution drift.
Monitoring recommendations for failed checks, stale features, and training-serving skew.
Ownership model for incident review, remediation, and recurring quality reports.

output_examples.input: Create standards for production data pipelines.
output: Checklist for tests, code review, deployment approvals, rollback plans, and runbooks.
Observability plan for latency, throughput, freshness, failure rate, and cost metrics.
Governance guidance for access control, audit logging, data retention, and sensitive data handling.

best_practices: Start with clear data contracts, ownership, freshness targets, and failure expectations.
Add validation, lineage, monitoring, and alerting before scaling pipeline volume.
Test transformations with realistic data sizes and document operational runbooks.
anti_patterns: Treating notebooks or one-time scripts as production pipelines without ownership.
Skipping schema validation, freshness checks, and backfill planning.
Optimizing infrastructure before measuring bottlenecks and business requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill run complete data pipelines?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and small templates. Users must implement and operate their own pipelines.

faq.question: Which tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Python, SQL, Spark, Airflow, dbt, Kafka, cloud platforms, monitoring tools, and modern data stack practices.

faq.question: Can it help with data quality?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers schema checks, completeness checks, anomaly detection, freshness monitoring, and alerting patterns.

faq.question: Is it suitable for streaming systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes high-level patterns for Kafka, stream processing, checkpointing, lag monitoring, and fault handling.

faq.question: Does it manage credentials or cloud resources?
faq.answer: No. Users must manage credentials, access controls, infrastructure, and compliance settings in their own environment.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
